mnemonics: CRT HATES LG: Cathode Ray Tubes, Heaters & HVAC, Appliances, Telecom, Entertainment & IT, Small Household, Lamps, Gadgets & Personal Devices. (Groups major e-waste sources by common categories)
keyPoints: E-waste is categorized by type (e.g., large household appliances, IT equipment, consumer electronics) and source (e.g., households, businesses, hospitals).
Primary sources include residential households, commercial establishments, government agencies, and industrial facilities.
The composition of e-waste is hazardous due to materials like lead, mercury, and cadmium, but also contains valuable recoverable resources like gold and copper.
commonMistakes: Assuming all electronics are the same type of waste; they are categorized differently (e.g., a refrigerator vs. a smartphone).
Overlooking 'invisible' e-waste from businesses and industries, focusing only on household consumer electronics.
Believing small items like phones are insignificant; they contribute a large volume and contain critical raw materials.
